evals.prompt: Store JWT in HttpOnly cookies in Next.js
evals.expected_output: In Server Action or Route Handler: (await cookies()).set('token', jwt, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax', maxAge: 86400 }); never use localStorage or sessionStorage

evals.prompt: Protect routes with middleware.ts in Next.js
evals.expected_output: export function middleware(req: NextRequest) { const token = req.cookies.get('token'); if (!token) return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url)); }; export const config = { matcher: ['/dashboard/:path*'] }

evals.prompt: Read auth tokens in Server Components
evals.expected_output: import { cookies } from 'next/headers'; const token = (await cookies()).get('token')?.value; validate token and get session; never pass raw token to Client Components

evals.prompt: Implement login flow with cookies in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Server Action validates credentials via DAL; sets HttpOnly cookie with signed JWT; redirects to /dashboard; middleware.ts verifies cookie presence on every protected request before rendering

evals.prompt: Why not use localStorage for tokens in Next.js?
evals.expected_output: localStorage is XSS-vulnerable (any script can read it); incompatible with Server Components (client-only API causes hydration issues); HttpOnly cookies are inaccessible to JS and work with RSC

evals.prompt: Set up session-based auth in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Use next-auth or iron-session; store encrypted session in HttpOnly cookie; validate in middleware.ts; read session in Server Components via getServerSession() or cookies(); no localStorage

should_not_trigger: JWT authentication in NestJS with Passport strategy
OAuth2 setup in a Go service
Firebase auth in a React Native app
